key_insight: Legitimate companies address you by name and never threaten account suspension in emails. If you feel rushed — stop and verify.

red_flags.label: Fake sender domain
red_flags.explanation: The email came from phishpulse-alert.com which is not an official PhishPulse domain.

red_flags.label: Artificial urgency
red_flags.explanation: Real security teams give you time to act. '30 minutes or permanent lock' is designed to panic you.

red_flags.label: Generic greeting
red_flags.explanation: The email says 'Dear User' — your real service knows your name.

attack_timeline.event: Landed on a fake PhishPulse login page
Attacker logged into your real account
Your account details and payment info extracted

key_insight: If an email demands action before you have time to think, it is designed to trap you. Step back and verify through official channels.

red_flags.label: Fake domain masquerading as Netflix
red_flags.explanation: billing.netflix@pulse-notifications.com is not a Netflix domain. Real Netflix emails come from netflix.com.

red_flags.label: Extreme urgency language
red_flags.explanation: 'SUSPENDED in 2 HOURS' and 'IMMEDIATELY' are pressure tactics to stop you from thinking clearly.

red_flags.label: SBI lookalike domain
red_flags.explanation: The KYC email came from pulse.com, not sbi.co.in. SBI never asks for KYC via email links.

attack_timeline.event: Landed on a Netflix-lookalike payment page
Entered credit card number, CVV, and expiry
